work: Haris et al. — Identifying strongly lensed GW signals
distinction_from_this_project: Pair identification rather than model-conditional absolute-magnification posterior inference

work: Li et al. — identifying lensed GWs
distinction_from_this_project: Population/rate framework rather than per-pair multimessenger magnification inference

work: Lo and Magaña Hernandez — Bayesian lensed-GW framework
inputs: GW strain and shared binary parameters
calibration: algorithm checks; no amortized coverage claim
distinction_from_this_project: GW-only joint identification framework; this project conditions absolute magnification on explicit EM lens information and reports calibration

inputs: GW strain and posterior from first image
outputs: conditional evidence and relative lensing parameters
distinction_from_this_project: Fast GW-only pair analysis; no deployable optical-lens observation model for absolute magnification

inputs: GW strain and parameterized lens model
distinction_from_this_project: General GW lens-parameter inference software rather than calibrated GW+EM galaxy-pair absolute-magnification benchmark

work: Goyal et al. — rapid ML identification
distinction_from_this_project: Pair classification rather than posterior estimation or calibration of magnifications

work: Seo et al. — dark-halo properties from lensed GWs
inputs: GW lensing observables plus lens axis ratio and lensed host images
distinction_from_this_project: Closest multimessenger lens-modeling direction; this project targets amortized per-pair magnification posteriors with explicit coverage and split protocol

work: Poon et al. — degeneracies in lensed GW cosmography
distinction_from_this_project: Provides the mass-sheet/distance identifiability constraints that this preregistration treats as mandatory conditioning

work: Dax et al. — DINGO importance sampling
distinction_from_this_project: Supplies validated NPE methodology; it does not solve lens magnification or multimessenger conditioning
Targets interference/overlapping regimes rather than well-separated galaxy-scale image pairs with optical lens information

work: Qin et al. — neural spline flows for microlensed GWs
distinction_from_this_project: Microlensing wave-optics inference; different regime and observables from separated galaxy macro images
Catalog search and candidate assessment rather than calibrated multimessenger absolute-magnification inference
Defines the public-search context but does not provide the proposed GW+EM posterior product
Methodological basis for independent SBC; not a gravitational-lensing competitor
